subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Differential Equation dy/dx = (y-1)/x² — Slope Field, Particular Solution with f(2)=0, Limit at Infinity

parts.prompt: On the axes provided, sketch a slope field for the given differential equation at the nine points indicated. (Note: Use the axes provided in the exam booklet.)
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $dy/dx = (y-1)/x^2$. At $y=1$ the slope is 0. For $y > 1$ slopes are positive; for $y < 1$ slopes are negative. Slopes are larger in magnitude near $x = 0$ than near $x = \pm 2$.

scoring_points.description: Zero slopes drawn correctly — at $y = 1$ (any $x \ne 0$), slope is $0$; the three points with $y = 1$ have horizontal segments

required_elements: Zero slopes drawn correctly — at $y = 1$ (any $x \ne 0$), slope is $0$; the three points with $y = 1$ have horizontal segments

scoring_points.description: All other slopes drawn with correct sign and relative steepness — slopes are positive where $y > 1$ and negative where $y < 1$; steeper when $|x|$ is small

required_elements: All other slopes drawn with correct sign
relative steepness — slopes are positive where $y > 1$
negative where $y < 1$; steeper when $|x|$ is small

parts.prompt: Find the particular solution $y = f(x)$ to the differential equation with the initial condition $f(2) = 0$.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Separation gives $\ln|y-1| = -1/x + C$. Using $f(2) = 0$: $\ln 1 = -1/2 + C \Rightarrow C = 1/2$. Then $y - 1 = ke^{-1/x}$ with $k = -e^{1/2}$, so $f(x) = 1 - e^{(1/2 - 1/x)}$, $x > 0$. Max 3/6 [1-2-0-0-0] if no constant of integration. 0/6 if no separation of variables.

parts.prompt: For the particular solution $y = f(x)$ described in part (b), find $\displaystyle\lim_{x \to \infty} f(x)$.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: As $x \to \infty$, $1/x \to 0$, so the exponent approaches $1/2$. $\lim_{x\to\infty} f(x) = 1 - e^{1/2} = 1 - \sqrt{e}$.
